Implementation of HIPC Initiative: Not Applicable
The de jure and de facto exchange rate arrangements in Qatar are conventional pegged
arrangements against the U.S. dollar. The Qatari riyal has been pegged to the U.S. dollar at
QR3.64 per U.S. dollar since July 2001, following an unofficial peg since June 1980. Qatar has
accepted the obligations under Article VIII, Sections 2(a), 3 and 4 of the IMF’s Articles of
Agreement, and maintains an exchange system that is free of restrictions on the making of
payments and transfers for current international transactions and multiple currency practices.
